sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: ALLO Stock Slumps As $175M Secondary Offering Hits Tape
feed.summary: Allogene Therapeutics (ALLO) stock slumped by 16.01% after announcing a $175M underwritten public offering of 87.5 million shares at $2.00 per share, significantly diluting existing shareholders. The offering, designed to fund clinical trials and R&D for its CAR-T pipeline, led to an 18% intraday price drop on increased volume. Traders are closely monitoring ALLO due to the dilution, insider selling intentions, and its high-burn, high-volatility biotech profile.

feed.title: Walmart is remodeling 12 Indiana stores with upgraded brands, services
feed.summary: Walmart is investing in remodeling 12 of its Indiana stores, including two in Indianapolis, as part of a nationwide modernization effort in 2026. The upgrades aim to enhance in-store and online shopping experiences by improving layouts, expanding healthy food options, introducing elevated brands, and offering faster services like express delivery. Stores will remain open during the remodel process.

feed.title: Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG) insiders report 10b5-1 sales of Common stock
feed.summary: Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG) has reported Form 144 sale notices for Common stock under 10b5-1 plans. The filings indicate sales of 669 shares on March 16, 2026, and 1,012 shares on February 17, 2026, associated with Glenn D Fogel and Amy J Roberts. These transactions highlight routine insider stock sales conducted through pre-arranged plans.

feed.title: 2025-2026 Harley-Davidson Softail Models Recalled For Rear Brake Issue
feed.summary: Harley-Davidson is recalling 16,994 units of its 2025-2026 Softail models, including the Street Bob, Heritage Classic, Low Rider S, and Low Rider ST, due to a potential rear brake fluid leak. The issue stems from the rear brake lines being positioned too close to the body control module (BCM), which can cause wear and lead to a loss of rear braking performance. Dealers will replace the BCM caddy and associated hardware to create more clearance, and inspect/replace damaged brake lines.

feed.title: Devon Energy on track to enter green territory after six straight sessions of losses
feed.summary: Devon Energy (DVN) is poised to end a six-session losing streak, with its shares rising 1.77% to $46.03. The energy company had previously seen a significant drop starting April 8, losing nearly 4.08% of its value. This uptick marks a potential shift for the stock after a period of decline.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley's bitcoin ETF hits $100M in week one
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley's Bitcoin ETF has achieved a significant milestone, accumulating $100 million in investments within its first week. This rapid growth highlights growing institutional interest in Bitcoin exchange-traded funds. The information is presented in a video format by CoinDesk.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Paolo Tonucci Sells Shares of Marex Group PLC
feed.summary: Paolo Tonucci, an insider at Marex Group PLC, sold 16,666 shares of the company on April 13, 2026, reducing his holding to 1,363,357 shares. This transaction follows a period with no insider buys but three insider sells, with the stock trading at $51.37 per share, giving the company a market capitalization of $3.74 billion and a P/E ratio of 12.98.

feed.title: Dividend reinvestment boosts GSK (NYSE: GSK) executive and director holdings
feed.summary: GSK plc reported that several senior executives and non-executive directors increased their equity holdings through dividend reinvestment plans. These acquisitions of Ordinary Shares and American Depositary Shares (ADS) on April 9 and April 14, 2026, were plan-based and tied to the declared dividend, rather than discretionary open-market purchases. The transactions marginally increase insider exposure to GSK equity, aligning leadership and board members with shareholders.

feed.title: International Paper's $360M NORPAC deal adds West Coast mill
feed.summary: International Paper (NYSE: IP) announced its agreement to acquire North Pacific Paper Company (NORPAC) for $360 million. This acquisition, subject to regulatory approval, will add NORPAC's Longview, Washington mill, its three machines, and approximately 500 employees, which annually produce about 1 million tons of containerboard and related grades. The deal aims to expand IP's West Coast capacity, enhance system flexibility, reduce costs, and meet the growing demand for recycled containerboard.

feed.title: Seagate lands on J.P. Morgan's 'Positive Catalyst Watch' due to strength of HDD market
feed.summary: J.P. Morgan has added Seagate Technology to its "Positive Catalyst Watch" list, citing the company's strong position in the HDD market. The firm believes Seagate is well-positioned to benefit from robust data center demand and favorable pricing trends. This update underlines a positive outlook for Seagate's future performance.

feed.title: What to Expect From Duke Energy's Q1 2026 Earnings Report
feed.summary: Duke Energy Corporation (DUK) is set to release its Q1 2026 earnings on May 5, with analysts projecting a 3.4% increase in EPS to $1.82 per share from the prior year. Despite trailing the S&P 500 and the Utilities Select Sector SPDR ETF, DUK has a "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts, who anticipate a 9.5% upside from its current price. The company recently invested $600,000 to bolster North Carolina's energy workforce.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Discusses Big Humana (HUM) Business
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Humana (HUM) due to its significant Medicare Advantage business, noting its stock performance after the Trump Administration announced increased Medicare Advantage payments for 2027. Humana's shares saw an 11% gain following this announcement, despite a prior downgrade by Barclays. The article suggests that while Humana has potential, some AI stocks may offer higher returns in a shorter timeframe.

feed.title: Can Jabil's Alliance With Sivers for AI Data Center Boost Its Shares?
feed.summary: Jabil Inc. has partnered with Sivers Semiconductors AB to develop ultra-high-speed, energy-efficient optical transceivers for AI data centers, aiming to meet future demands for faster networking. This collaboration, along with other partnerships, solidifies Jabil's role in advanced AI infrastructure development, competing with companies like Celestica and Flex. Despite past share underperformance, Jabil exhibits strong financial estimates for fiscal 2026 and 2027, earning it a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy).

feed.title: Texas Instruments Hit With Patent Lawsuit by Lock Semiconductor
feed.summary: Lock Semiconductor LLC has filed a patent infringement lawsuit against Texas Instruments Inc. (TI) alleging the company used protected methods in its microcontroller and processor products. The lawsuit, filed in the US District Court for the Eastern District of Texas, claims TI was aware of the infringed patents (Nos. 7,952,401, 8,117,400, and 9,355,280) through its competition with original patent owners Freescale Semiconductor Inc. and NXP Semiconductors NV.

feed.title: Sands Surpasses the Primary People, Communities and Planet Ambitions Set for Its 2021-2025 Reporting Period
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands has exceeded its 2021-2025 environmental, social, and governance (ESG) goals, including investments in workforce development, community volunteer hours, and carbon emissions reduction. The company invested over $270 million in workforce development and logged more than 290,000 volunteer hours. Sands also reduced its scope 1 and 2 emissions by 54% from a 2018 base year, surpassing its targets.

feed.title: +1.89% for Analog Devices stock as buyers keep price at top of weekly range
feed.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) stock gained 1.89%, trading at the top of its weekly range. The stock is in a strong bullish trend, well above major moving averages, with momentum indicators confirming sustained buying interest despite approaching overbought conditions. Analysts project a trading range of $347.70 to $362.20 for the coming week, with a high probability of further increases toward its 52-week high of $363.20.

feed.title: Home BancShares (NYSE:HOMB) Shares Gap Down - Time to Sell?
feed.summary: Home BancShares (NYSE:HOMB) experienced a share price gap down, opening at $26.70 after closing at $27.67, and trading down about 1.9%. Analysts currently hold a consensus "Hold" rating with an average target price of $32.40. The company's Q1 EPS matched estimates, but revenue missed, and it maintains a quarterly dividend of $0.21.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: BNY Mellon surpasses Q1 2026 forecasts with strong EPS
feed.summary: BNY Mellon reported strong first-quarter 2026 earnings, with EPS of $2.25 surpassing forecasts by 16.58% and revenue reaching $5.41 billion against a projected $5.17 billion. The company attributes its performance to record revenue growth in Securities Services and Market and Wealth Services, significant investments in AI and technology, and strong client engagement. Despite a slight pre-market stock dip, BNY Mellon remains near its 52-week high and increased its full-year 2026 revenue and net interest income outlook.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Milton Morris Sells Shares of Nordson Corp (NDSN)
feed.summary: Milton Morris, a Director at Nordson Corp (NDSN), sold 245 shares of the company on April 15, 2026, bringing his total ownership to 2,656 shares. This transaction is part of a trend of nine insider sells and no buys at Nordson Corp over the past year. The company's stock is currently trading at $273.87, which is considered fairly valued based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Exelon (EXC) Shares Decline Following PECO Rate Filing Withdrawa
feed.summary: Exelon (EXC) shares fell by 1% to $47.35 after its regulated utility, PECO, withdrew its rate proceedings. This development has raised investor concerns about the regulatory environment and its potential impact on Exelon's revenue and operational strategies. Despite a moderate GF Score™ of 66/100 and strengths in profitability and growth, the company's financial strength rating of 3/10 suggests caution for investors.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks founder applies to invest in California bank
feed.summary: Nir Zuk, founder of Palo Alto Networks, submitted an application to acquire voting shares in DMG Bancshares, the holding company for Liberty Bank in Irvine, California. Zuk's investment could serve as a platform to showcase the use of artificial intelligence tools in financial services, following his previous venture with an AI-based banking platform in Israel. This move aligns with a broader trend of tech billionaires and firms, including Palmer Luckey, OpenAI, and Elon Musk, expanding into the financial services sector.

feed.title: Pfizer-Backed EV-209 Trial Targets Bladder Preservation in High-Need Cancer Segment
feed.summary: Pfizer Inc. (PFE) has provided an update on its Phase 2 EV-209 study, which aims to evaluate a combination of enfortumab vedotin and pembrolizumab for bladder preservation in patients with muscle-invasive bladder cancer. This open-label, single-arm trial seeks to control tumors and allow patients to avoid bladder removal surgery, thereby improving their quality of life. The study, led by Astellas Pharma with Pfizer as a key collaborator, could significantly impact oncology treatment by establishing a bladder-preserving option in a high-need cancer segment.

feed.title: Bruno Mars Pokes Fun at Gambling Debt Rumors on Opening Night of Tour
feed.summary: Bruno Mars addressed ongoing rumors about his gambling debt during the opening night of his "The Romantic Tour" with Anderson .Paak in Las Vegas. He and .Paak lightheartedly joked about the alleged debt, with Mars declaring he was "debt free" and had "paid the casino off a long time ago." This follows a busy period for Mars, which included opening a cocktail lounge and being honored with a Las Vegas street renaming and key to the city.

feed.title: +1.89% for Analog Devices stock as price pushes to upper weekly range
feed.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) stock saw a 1.89% increase, pushing its price to the upper weekly range. The company's stock is trading near all-time highs with strong bullish momentum, though technical indicators suggest overbought conditions. Analysts project a trading range of $347.50 to $360.50 for the week, with further upside expected if $360.50 is breached.

feed.title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC has issued $578,000 in Auto Callable Contingent Interest Notes, fully and unconditionally guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co. These notes are linked to the performance of the Russell 2000® Index, the VanEck® Semiconductor ETF, and the State Street® SPDR® S&P® Regional Banking ETF. Investors can receive contingent interest payments if the underlying assets meet certain conditions, but risk losing principal if conditions are not met.

feed.title: Graphic Packaging International launches restructuring, layoffs
feed.summary: Graphic Packaging International has initiated a restructuring that includes layoffs across various departments and locations, impacting corporate and operations roles. This move follows months of foreshadowing by company executives, including former CEO Mike Doss and new CEO Robbert Rietbroek, who cited weak market conditions, oversupply, and the need to cut $60 million in costs for 2026. The restructuring also comes amid past issues with higher-than-anticipated capital expenditures at the Waco, Texas, mill and internal control deficiencies related to board approvals for spending.

feed.title: Coca-Cola vs. Johnson & Johnson: The Safest Dividend on Wall Street Might Surprise You
feed.summary: This article compares Coca-Cola (KO) and Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) as Dividend Kings, analyzing their latest financial results and future prospects to determine which offers a "safer" dividend. Coca-Cola relies on its Zero Sugar unit growth and pricing power, while Johnson & Johnson's performance is driven by its pharmaceutical and MedTech segments, particularly new drug approvals. While JNJ shows higher YTD price performance due to pipeline growth, KO is highlighted for its stability and capital preservation.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Shows How Dividend Growth Can Help Investors Recover Faster After Big Stock Declines
feed.summary: This article explains how dividend growth, coupled with strong business fundamentals, can accelerate investor recovery after significant stock price declines. It emphasizes distinguishing between market-wide corrections and business-specific failures to inform investment decisions. The piece highlights Texas Instruments as an example, illustrating how dividends and robust fundamentals can alter the recovery trajectory after sharp stock drops.

feed.title: Public Storage’s New US$500 Million Notes: What Does This Say About PSA’s Long-Term Capital Play?
feed.summary: Public Storage (PSA) recently completed a US$500 million fixed-rate senior notes issuance due 2035 through its operating subsidiary. These funds are intended to repay a revolving credit facility and support general corporate needs, including acquisitions, development, and mortgage lending for its self-storage platform. The debt raise provides short-term capital flexibility but the company's long-term success still hinges on its ability to execute growth strategies amidst industry oversupply and evolving market conditions.

feed.title: New Royal Caribbean Group Foundation scales up
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has launched a new foundation to formalize and scale its philanthropic efforts globally, focusing on education, community well-being, and environmental stewardship. The Foundation's inaugural pledge supports an emergency medicine residency program at Jackson Health System in South Florida. This initiative builds on the company's existing "SEA the Future" framework, which has already contributed significantly to scholarships, conservation, and disaster relief worldwide.

feed.title: [Form 4] CORNING INC /NY Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Jordana Daryl Kammerud, Senior Vice President at Corning Incorporated, recently exercised stock-based awards. On April 15, 2026, she converted 12,845 performance share units and 5,670 restricted stock units into common shares. A total of 9,018 shares were withheld at $168.27 each to cover tax obligations, resulting in her direct ownership of 12,269 common shares and additional unvested units.

feed.title: Corning Inc Stock (GLW) Moved Down by 3.31% on Apr 16: Facts Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Corning Inc (GLW) stock decreased by 3.31% on April 16, influenced by a JPMorgan downgrade from "Overweight" to "Neutral" due to valuation concerns, despite an increased price target. The stock's recent significant rally, up 285% since April 2025, has also led to profit-taking by investors. While recent insider selling and upcoming Q1 2026 earnings add to investor caution, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus for GLW, seeing its Optical Communications segment as a key growth driver.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Inc Stock (TXN) Moved Up by 3.28% on Apr 16: Facts Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) stock rose 3.28% due to a recent analyst upgrade, its strategic position in the growing semiconductor market, and implemented price increases on core components. The company's collaboration with Nvidia and a strong industry outlook, including Gartner's projected $1.3 trillion semiconductor revenue by 2026, further bolstered investor confidence. Technical indicators show a buy signal from MACD, while the company's annual meeting may have also provided positive affirmation.

feed.title: John Deere agrees to $99M settlement in right-to-repair case
feed.summary: John Deere has agreed to a $99 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit alleging it restricted access to equipment repairs, with the funds intended to compensate farmers and others who paid for agricultural equipment repairs since January 2018. The settlement, awaiting court approval, would also expand access to repair tools and diagnostic capabilities. This case is part of a wider "right to repair" debate, and while a similar lawsuit against Harley-Davidson was dismissed, Deere continues to face a lawsuit from the Federal Trade Commission regarding its repair practices.

feed.title: ICON plc stock (IE0005711209): Why its clinical trial dominance matters more now for U.S. investors?
feed.summary: ICON plc is a crucial contract research organization (CRO) providing end-to-end clinical trial services, positioning it as a strategic investment for those tracking healthcare growth, particularly in the U.S. market. The company benefits from surging biopharma R&D spending and an outsourcing trend among major pharmaceutical firms, offering a full-service model that includes decentralized trials and advanced analytics. ICON's strong market position, technology-driven solutions, and focus on high-need therapeutic areas make it a resilient stock amidst changing economic and regulatory landscapes.

feed.title: iShares iBonds Dec 2031 term treasury ETF declares monthly distribution of $0.0615
feed.summary: The iShares iBonds Dec 2031 Term Treasury ETF (IBTL) has declared a monthly distribution of $0.0615 per share. This announcement offers information for investors tracking the fund's regular payouts.

feed.title: Flexible Solutions International targets $50M-$60M annual revenue run rate from food contracts by 2027 as plant expansions advance
feed.summary: NULL

feed.title: L3Harris Technologies plans $1.3B solid rocket motor expansion in Virginia
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies is investing $1.3 billion to expand its solid rocket motor manufacturing capacity in Orange County, Virginia, building on a previous $41.2 million investment. This expansion will establish an advanced propulsion campus, creating over 350 jobs and supporting multiple Department of Defense programs. The move aligns with the DOD's increased demand for weapons systems and the company's new missile solutions business, which seeks an IPO in the second half of 2026.

feed.title: Tractor Supply (NASDAQ:TSCO) Receives "Buy" Rating from DA Davidson
feed.summary: DA Davidson reissued a "Buy" rating on Tractor Supply (NASDAQ:TSCO) with a $60 price objective, suggesting a 34% upside. While analysts are mixed, the average rating is "Moderate Buy" with a $58.09 price target. The company recently missed quarterly earnings and revenue estimates, and notable insider selling has been observed despite high institutional ownership.

feed.title: IDACORP Schedules First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release & Conference Call
feed.summary: IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE:IDA) announced it will report its first quarter 2026 results on Thursday, April 30, before market open. The company will host an analyst conference call at 2:30 p.m. Mountain Time (4:30 p.m. Eastern Time) on the same day to discuss the earnings. Interested parties can listen via a live webcast or by phone, with a replay available on the company's website.

feed.title: Steel producers tease Q1 earnings surge, citing higher prices
feed.summary: U.S. steel producers Nucor Corp. and Steel Dynamics are anticipating a significant surge in their first-quarter earnings, attributed to increased sales volumes and higher prices. This boost is largely a result of Section 232 tariffs on metal imports, which have redirected demand towards domestic steel and enabled prices to surpass raw material costs. Analyst Seth Goldstein notes that these conditions are expected to persist through most of 2026, depending on the duration of tariffs and geopolitical stability.

feed.title: FIAT Stock Price | YieldMax Short COIN Option Income Strategy ETF
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information about the YieldMax Short COIN Option Income Strategy ETF (FIAT), including its current stock price, trading exchange, and key financial metrics. It offers a comprehensive overview of the ETF's holdings, historical data, dividends, and technical analysis indicators. The report also addresses common FAQs about FIAT, such as its performance, expense ratio, and dividend yield, and details its investment strategy.

feed.title: HIVE Digital Technologies (NASDAQ:HIVE) Shares Gap Down - Here's What Happened
feed.summary: HIVE Digital Technologies (NASDAQ:HIVE) shares gapped down significantly, opening at $2.29 after recently closing at $2.47, and last trading at $2.1650, representing a 13.2% drop on high volume. This decline follows several brokerages trimming their price targets for HIVE, though the consensus remains a "Moderate Buy." Institutional investors hold approximately 24% of the company, which specializes in mining digital assets like Bitcoin and Ethereum.

feed.title: Walmart to remodel 58 Florida stores, including Tampa site
feed.summary: Walmart plans to remodel 58 stores across Florida in 2026, including a Tampa location at 2701 E. Fletcher Ave., to enhance both in-store and online shopping experiences. The upgrades will focus on store layouts, technology, and service offerings to support faster delivery and expanded digital tools. This investment builds on $1.5 billion Walmart has put into Florida over the past five years, reflecting shifts in consumer demand for convenience and digital integration.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges PayPal Holdings,
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against PayPal Holdings, Inc. and its officers for alleged violations of federal securities laws. The lawsuit claims that PayPal made false and misleading statements regarding its revenue outlook and growth strategy between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to join the case, with a lead plaintiff deadline of April 20, 2026.

feed.title: Gartner, Inc. (IT) Shareholders Who Lost Money Have Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay Wolke & Rotter LLP has announced a securities fraud class action lawsuit against Gartner, Inc. (NYSE: IT). The lawsuit alleges that Gartner failed to disclose challenges in meeting consulting revenue targets or maintaining CV growth between February 4, 2025, and February 2, 2026, leading to materially misleading statements. Shareholders who lost money have until May 18, 2026, to participate as lead plaintiffs.

feed.title: Corning (GLW) SVP & CHRO exercises equity awards, now holds 42,951 shares
feed.summary: Corning's Senior Vice President & CHRO, Michelle L. Gullo, exercised equity awards, converting performance share units and restricted stock units into 13,149 shares. Although 6,198 shares were withheld for tax obligations, her direct ownership of common stock increased to 42,951 shares. Gullo also retains unvested performance and restricted stock units scheduled to vest between April 2026 and April 2029.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: Apollo Global Management, Inc. (APO)
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay Wolke & Rotter LLP is alerting investors of Apollo Global Management, Inc. (APO) about an upcoming May 1, 2026, deadline to file as lead plaintiff in a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Apollo Global made materially false and misleading statements to investors regarding ties between its leadership, including CEO Marc Rowan and former CEO Leon Black, and Jeffrey Epstein. The firm claims Apollo's stock price dropped significantly after news reports detailed these connections, contradicting the company's previous assertions.

feed.title: NVDA vs. AMAT: Which Semiconductor Stock is a Better Buy Now?
feed.summary: This article analyzes NVIDIA (NVDA) and Applied Materials (AMAT), two major players in the semiconductor industry, to determine which is a better investment amid the AI-driven upcycle. While AMAT benefits from strong demand for wafer fabrication equipment due to AI-driven capital expenditure and memory production shifts, it faces challenges from U.S.-China tensions and competition. NVIDIA, however, is highlighted as a stronger buy due to its direct role in AI creation through its dominant Data Center business, robust financial performance, and favorable valuation despite geopolitical concerns.

feed.title: General Mills Issues €1.7 Billion Long-Term Subordinated Notes
feed.summary: General Mills (GIS) is issuing €1.7 billion in long-dated junior subordinated notes to support its capital structure and funding flexibility. The offering consists of €1.0 billion of 4.750% Series A notes and €700 million of 5.250% Series B notes, both maturing in 2056. TipRanks' AI Analyst Spark rates GIS as Neutral, citing weakening financial performance and a bearish technical setup, but acknowledges a supportive dividend yield and plans for margin recovery.
